title: Rough-variance scaling identification theorem
assumptions: The volatility driver admits a Volterra representation with local singularity exponent H-1/2.
The observed realized-variance proxy is asymptotically consistent up to lower-order noise distortion.
Empirical scope anchored to SPY US Equity.
Higher-order remainder is lower order than Delta^(2H)

tasks.name: variance_scaling_factorization
tasks.rationale: Checks algebraic coherence of the scaling-law form used in the theorem candidate.

tasks.name: variance_term_nonnegative
tasks.rationale: Under positive coefficient and positive scale assumptions, the scaling contribution should remain nonnegative.

tasks.name: increment_variance_nonnegative
tasks.rationale: Variance contribution must remain nonnegative under the roughness scaling ansatz.

tasks.name: scaling_at_origin_consistency
tasks.rationale: Checks a simple origin-consistency proxy for higher-order remainder terms in the small-scale expansion.

tasks.name: exponent_additivity
tasks.rationale: Provides a reusable exponent-composition identity for scaling arguments.

notes: These tasks do not prove the full theorem.
They turn the current scaling-law candidate into executable symbolic consistency checks.
The next layer should connect these tasks to verification_engine and theorem_refinement_engine.
